isolation.boundary: Local repo and local services only; no production logic modified.

assertions.title: Insight rules fire based on metric status thresholds

steps.action: Run targeted VAL-COMP assertion suite in insights.test.ts
steps.expected: Rule firing/non-firing behavior and anomaly threshold behavior pass for elevated_rhr, low_sleep_score, suppressed_hrv, and multi_metric_deviation
steps.observed: PASS: 17 tests under "VAL-COMP-010: individual rules fire correctly" passed (critical/warning trigger and normal/good non-trigger checks, plus anomaly_score threshold checks).

steps.action: Run targeted severity mapping tests in insights.test.ts
steps.expected: critical -> warning and warning -> info mapping for metric rules; multi_metric_deviation warning severity
steps.observed: PASS: all 5 "severity mapping" tests passed, including elevated_rhr, low_sleep_score, suppressed_hrv, and multi_metric_deviation severity expectations.

assertions.title: Insight priority ordering, max 3 cap, and dismissal exclusion

steps.action: Run targeted VAL-COMP assertion suite in insights.test.ts
steps.expected: Top-3 priority order enforced, cap at 3, and dismissed types excluded with lower-priority backfill
steps.observed: PASS: 8 tests under "VAL-COMP-011: priority ordering, max 3 cap, dismissal exclusion" passed, including ordering (5,10,20), cap behavior, dismissal skip/backfill, and empty outcomes when all dismissed.

steps.action: Run targeted VAL-COMP assertion suite in insights.test.ts
steps.expected: Rules fire only for allowed viewTypes and do not fire for disallowed viewTypes (e.g., trend)
steps.observed: PASS: 7 tests under "VAL-COMP-012: viewType scoping" passed, confirming elevated_rhr/low_sleep_score/suppressed_hrv do not fire on trend and allowed-scope behavior remains correct.

frictions.description: Severity mapping checks required a second targeted run because those tests are grouped under "severity mapping" rather than assertion-ID-tagged describe names.
frictions.resolution: Executed a second scoped vitest command (-t "severity mapping") and captured separate log/exit code artifacts.
